evals.prompt: How do I improve Angular change detection performance?
evals.expected_output: Use ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush on all components. Use Signals for state — they integrate with zoneless Angular. Avoid function calls in templates; use computed() instead. Prepare for zoneless by avoiding Zone.runOutsideAngular hacks.

evals.prompt: How do I use @defer to lazy load a heavy component?
evals.expected_output: Wrap heavy components with @defer (on viewport) { <HeavyComponent /> } @placeholder { <Spinner /> }. Use triggers: on viewport, on interaction, on idle, when condition. @defer creates a separate lazy-loaded chunk automatically.

evals.prompt: How do I use NgOptimizedImage for LCP optimization in Angular?
evals.expected_output: Import NgOptimizedImage and replace with . Add priority attribute for LCP images. This enables lazy loading, responsive srcset, and preconnect hints automatically.

evals.prompt: Should I avoid function calls in Angular templates?
evals.expected_output: Yes — {{ calculate() }} re-evaluates on every change detection cycle. Replace with a computed() signal: displayValue = computed(() => this.calculate()). Then use {{ displayValue() }} in the template. Computed() caches until dependencies change.

evals.prompt: How do I prepare Angular for zoneless rendering?
evals.expected_output: Use Signals for all reactive state instead of Zone.runOutsideAngular hacks. Set ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush on all components. Avoid setTimeout/setInterval side effects — use effect() or RxJS timer instead. Opt into provideExperimentalZonelessChangeDetection().

evals.prompt: How do I use trackBy in Angular @for loops?
evals.expected_output: Use @for (item of items; track item.id) { } — the track expression replaces trackBy. Always provide a stable unique identifier (e.g., item.id). This prevents Angular from destroying and recreating DOM nodes on list updates.
